data.title: NY, IL issue executive order banning state workers from prediction markets. Governor Hochul slams Trump admin for lack of ethical standards.
data.description: Governor Kathy Hochul of New York lambasted the Trump administration for failing to enforce effective ethical guidelines to prevent insider trading in prediction markets. Hochul expressed disappointment in the lack of meaningful standards, signaling a potential loophole in the regulatory framework. This critique comes amid growing concerns about the integrity of prediction markets, where individuals trade based on confidential information. The Governor's remarks underscore the urgency of addressing this issue, as it could have significant implications for market transparency and fairness.

data.title: MiCA deadline nears: EU's regulation requires compliance by July 1, 2026. Small crypto firms must adjust as costs soar.
data.description: New MiCA regulation from the EU requires compliance by July 1, 2026. This is putting pressure on smaller crypto companies to either adjust or leave the market due to increasing costs and consolidation.

data.title: Pacific Prime Engages in 2026 Human Capital Conference on AI and Leadership
data.description: A prominent insurance and employee benefits solutions provider, Pacific Prime, sponsored the 2026 Human Capital Conference in Beijing with the theme "The Augmented Leader: Navigating Complexity with AI." The event, organized by the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China at the Four Seasons Hotel Beijing, gathered executives, HR experts, and thought leaders to explore AI's impact on leadership and organizational changes. Pacific Prime's participation was led by Senior Cor, showcasing their commitment to this evolving sector.

data.title: Arbitrum's rally pauses at $0.135 - consider selling before $0.10 support level. Retail FOMO vs. institutional selling pressure.
data.description: After experiencing a bear market bounce, Arbitrum reached a resistance level set at $0.135, indicating mounting distribution signals. There are predictions that the target price will drop to $0.10 within the next three weeks due to the clash between retail fear of missing out (FOMO) and institutional selling pressure.

data.title: Investors predict APT to surge to $1.20 before plunging to $0.75 with a 35% drop after hitting $0.97.
data.description: Aptos is displaying typical distribution patterns, currently priced at $0.97 per share, amid derivatives positioning indicating a significant institutional sell-off. Analysts anticipate a minor recovery to hit resistance at $1.20 before experiencing a sharp 35% drop to $0.75.

data.title: Not Just Eco-Friendly: PITAKA Makes Sustainability Measurable, One Tree at a Time
data.description: On April 22, 2026, in Hong Kong, PITAKA is taking a significant step towards sustainability this Earth Day. They have decided to go beyond simply promoting the message of sustainability by taking real, tangible actions to help the environment. PITAKA has joined forces with Tree-Nation for their latest initiative. For every sale of the new "Breath of Veins" phone case, a mangrove tree will be planted. This initiative aims to support environmental conservation efforts and promote sustainability.

data.title: Russian parliament approves crypto bill requiring licensed intermediaries and penalties for unregistered platforms. #cryptocurrency #regulation
data.description: The Russian parliament has advanced a new crypto bill that requires licensed intermediaries and imposes harsh penalties on unregistered platforms. This legislation aims to regulate the crypto market and protect investors. The bill specifies that all crypto transactions must go through licensed intermediaries, and failure to comply will result in fines of up to $45,000 or imprisonment for up to seven years. This move is part of a larger effort to bring more oversight and control to the growing cryptocurrency industry in Russia.

data.title: Bitcoin Cash aims for $500 breakout ahead of May network upgrade. Price surpasses $465 resistance, on track to hit target in 3 weeks as institutional adoption grows.
data.description: Bitcoin Cash has successfully surpassed the crucial resistance level of $465, receiving increasing interest from institutional investors in anticipation of the Layla upgrade scheduled for May 15. The target price set is $500 within the next three weeks, further fueled by the squeezing of short positions.
